The last charging point would be at our basecamp in Lohajung.

extracted_items.value: Concrete toilets

evidence.quote_or_summary: The guest house at Lohajung will have concrete toilets.

extracted_items.value: Toilet tents (2-4 depending on group size) with deep pit dry toilets; soil mound and shovel provided; plain toilet paper required

evidence.quote_or_summary: During the trek, toilet tents will be set up along each campsite. There will be 2 to 4 of these toilet tents depending on the size of the group. A toilet tent will have a deep pit, where one can answer nature’s call. There will be a mound of soil and a shovel to cover it up. These are dry toilets, where you’ll have to use toilet paper.

extracted_items.value: Wet wipes are prohibited as they are not biodegradable

evidence.quote_or_summary: Please use plain toilet paper and refrain from using wet wipes since these are not biodegradable.

extracted_items.value: Campsites are pitched near water sources; 2 litres is sufficient for a day's trek

evidence.quote_or_summary: Our campsites are pitched near water sources. For your day’s trek, two litres of water should be enough.

extracted_items.value: One water source available on Day 2 and Day 5

evidence.quote_or_summary: On Day 2 and Day 5, you will find one water source to refill your water bottles.

extracted_items.value: Signals at Lohajung for most networks; intermittent in mountains

evidence.quote_or_summary: You will get mobile signals at Lohajung for most networks. Signals will be intermittent in the mountains.

extracted_items.value: Last charging point at basecamp in Lohajung; power cuts possible

evidence.quote_or_summary: The last charging point would be at our basecamp in Lohajung. However, come prepared for the possibility of power cuts at Lohajung.

extracted_items.value: Rishikesh

evidence.quote_or_summary: Rishikesh is your best bet to make last-minute cash withdrawals.

extracted_items.value: Rs 4,000-5,000 recommended for transport to basecamp, rental gear deposit, and personal expenses en route; more if visiting attractions after the trek

evidence.quote_or_summary: We advise you to carry around Rs 4-5k in cash to pay for your transport to the basecamp, for rental gear deposit and personal expenses en route to the basecamp. If you are planning to visit other attractions after the trek, we advise you to carry more.

extracted_items.value: Meals, accommodations, permits, and related expenses covered by Indiahikes once at basecamp

evidence.quote_or_summary: If you’re an Indiahikes trekker, there is no major need for cash as once you arrive at the base camp. All meals, accommodations, permits, and related expenses are taken care of by Indiahikes.

extracted_items.value: Extra luggage can be left at guest house in Lohajung; valuables should not be left

evidence.quote_or_summary: Since you will be starting and returning to the guest house at Lohajung, it is possible for you to leave some luggage behind, if required. However, make sure you do not leave any valuables - Indiahikes is not responsible for loss or damage to any belongings.
